verdict_reasoning: STIM1 is a single-pass (type I) transmembrane protein resident primarily in the ER membrane, where its luminal EF-hand senses Ca2+ store depletion. Upon store depletion it oligomerizes and translocates to ER-PM junctions, where its cytosolic SOAR/CAD domain directly gates the plasma-membrane channel Orai1. Critically, STIM1 also has a short extracellular N-terminal domain (the EF-hand/SAM region) that faces the ER lumen and, at ER-PM puncta, is accessible from the extracellular space at the plasma membrane fraction. Multiple studies and therapeutic programs have targeted the extracellular/luminal domain of STIM1 at the cell surface. Its own TM domain anchors it in the membrane; no secondary carrier is required. This qualifies as a bona-fide type-I TM protein with a stably membrane-anchored conformation accessible from the outer leaflet context, making the verdict 'yes'.
